answers: Start beekeeping: get a hive, protective gear, smoker, and hive tool. Spring is the best time to begin with a nuc (nucleus colony).
Beekeeping basics: inspect hives weekly, manage for pests (varroa mites), ensure queen is laying, and harvest honey in late summer.
Honey production needs patience — a new hive may take a full year before you harvest. Bees need flowers within 3 miles.
Modhu chas er jonno: moghmachi palan koro, ful bagan rakho kachhe, niyomito hive check koro, ar modhu shongroho koro shomoy moto.
